paragraphs.text: I strongly support the Digital Fairness Act and urge the European Commission to ensure that it includes clear protections for consumers who purchase digital games and content. Currently, when we buy a game, we often dont truly own it. Publishers canand frequently dodisable access, shut down servers, or remove purchased titles entirely from libraries. This is not only deeply unfair, but also undermines the basic principle of ownership. Consumers are paying for a product that can be revoked at any time, with no recourse or compensation. This practice: Erodes trust in digital markets Creates financial loss for consumers Unilaterally shifts control to companies, even after a sale is made The Digital Fairness Act must address this by: Enforcing post-purchase access rights: If a game was sold, it should remain playableoffline or via backup accesseven if its delisted or servers shut down. Banning unilateral deactivation of paid content unless a full refund or offline alternative is offered. Guaranteeing transparency: Clear, upfront terms around how long access will be available, and what happens if the product is pulled. Digital ownership should mean the same thing as physical ownershipif I buy a game, I should be able to play it. This is a basic consumer right that needs to be upheld in EU law. Lets end the era of licensed until further notice. Thank you for moving this legislation forward.
